title: Rivian Q1 2024 Earnings Report
text: Rivian reported Q1 2024 earnings with revenue of $1.2 billion, matching Wall Street expectations and up 80% year-over-year. EPS loss was $1.48/share, exceeding projections by $0.31. Gross profit loss decreased due to increased vehicle production and deliveries. The company produced its 100,000th vehicle at its Normal facility and completed manufacturing plant retooling upgrades. The company unveiled its new midsize platform supporting the R2, R3, and R3X models. CEO RJ Scaringe stated the results set a strong foundation for the remainder of the year as the company focuses on continued demand generation, delivering cost and plant efficiency improvements. Rivian's stock declined $0.77 per share on May 7, 2024, a decline that persisted through after-hours trading. The company is pursuing an $827 million incentive package from Illinois for manufacturing expansion and infrastructure improvements.
